meta.title: Insurance Licensing and Contracting
meta.catalog: Sircon (Vertafore) — Producer Lifecycle Management Best-Practice Catalog

steps.activity: Onboarding request submitted by Regional Sales Manager
steps.output: Candidate details, product lines, territory and proposed date
Status, expiry and CE compliance confirmed via regulator portal; recorded in AgentOS
Criminal, credit and conduct check; Compliance reviews adverse findings within 3 days
Code of Conduct, AML and Product Knowledge via LMS; complete before filing
Standard agreement signed via DocuSign; filed in DMS
Filed via regulator portal; confirmation recorded; 5–15 business days processing

steps.activity: Appointment confirmed; agent activated in PAS and CRM
steps.output: Agent record created; product authorities, territory and commission tier set

steps.activity: Renewal alert triggered 90 days before expiry
steps.output: Agent and Licensing Specialist notified by email
CE transcript reviewed; shortfall notified with required hours and providers
Filed at least 30 days before expiry; renewal fee paid from licensing budget
New expiry date updated in AgentOS and PAS; agent notified

steps.activity: Termination request initiated (voluntary or for cause)
steps.output: Reason code recorded; final commission run initiated; open cases reviewed
Termination notice executed per agreement terms; copy filed in DMS
Appointment withdrawal filed within 30 days of effective termination

steps.activity: Agent record deactivated in PAS and CRM
steps.output: System access revoked; policy book transferred to replacement agent

swim_phases.name: New agent appointment
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–7 · verify, screen, train, appoint
swim_phases.note: Appointments require regulator verification, background screening, mandatory training and an executed agreement before filing.

swim_phases.name: Licence renewal
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 8–11 · alerts, CE, filing
swim_phases.note: Renewals are driven by a 90-day alert; CE shortfalls are notified before the renewal is filed at least 30 days pre-expiry.

swim_phases.name: Termination
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 12–15 · terminate, file, deactivate
swim_phases.note: Terminations execute the agreement, file the regulator withdrawal within 30 days, and revoke access with book transfer.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-005 against the Sircon producer lifecycle catalog
detail_slides: Steps 1–7 vs Sircon producer lifecycle catalog
Steps 8–12 vs Sircon producer lifecycle catalog
Steps 13–14 vs Sircon producer lifecycle catalog

items.name: Onboarding request capture
items.detail: Structured onboarding request with product lines and territory aligns with Sircon producer onboarding intake.

items.name: Regulator licence verification
items.detail: Licence status is verified at appointment via the regulator portal, but checks are point-in-time. Sircon maintains continuous, real-time licence/appointment synchronisation with regulator (PDB/NIPR) data.

items.name: Background screening
items.detail: Criminal, credit and conduct screening with Compliance review aligns with Sircon background-check workflow.

items.name: Continuous compliance monitoring
items.detail: Screening occurs only at appointment. Sircon continuously monitors adverse actions, watchlists and licence standing between renewals — without it, mid-term disqualifying events go undetected.

items.name: Pre-appointment training gate
items.detail: Mandatory AML/conduct/product training before filing aligns with Sircon readiness requirements.

items.name: Distribution agreement execution
items.detail: DocuSign execution with DMS filing and version control aligns with Sircon contracting/onboarding.

items.name: Appointment filing
items.detail: Filing is performed manually through the regulator portal. Sircon automates appointment/termination filings via direct NIPR/PDB integration, cutting cycle time and transcription error.

items.name: Agent activation & authorities
items.detail: Agent activation with product authorities, territory and commission tier aligns with Sircon producer setup.

items.name: Renewal alerting
items.detail: 90-day renewal alerting aligns with Sircon licence-expiry management.

items.name: CE tracking
items.detail: CE transcripts are reviewed manually. Sircon auto-syncs CE completions from providers and flags shortfalls without manual transcript review.

items.name: Renewal filing
items.detail: Renewal filed at least 30 days before expiry with fee payment aligns with Sircon renewal processing.

items.name: Ready-to-sell enforcement
items.detail: Selling during a lapse is detected after the fact and reviewed for rescission. Sircon enforces a real-time 'ready-to-sell' gate that blocks unlicensed/unappointed sales at point of sale.

items.name: Termination & withdrawal filing
items.detail: For-cause/voluntary termination with regulator withdrawal within 30 days aligns with Sircon termination.

items.name: Deactivation & book transfer
items.detail: Access revocation and policy-book transfer on deactivation align with Sircon offboarding.

critical_missing.title: Continuous compliance monitoring
critical_missing.detail: Screening and licence checks occur only at appointment and renewal. The catalog monitors adverse actions, watchlists and licence standing continuously, so mid-term disqualifying events are caught early.

critical_missing.title: Ready-to-sell enforcement at point of sale
critical_missing.detail: Sales during a lapse are only detected after the fact and reviewed for rescission. The catalog enforces a real-time ready-to-sell gate that blocks unlicensed/unappointed sales.

critical_missing.title: Automated regulator filing (NIPR/PDB)
critical_missing.detail: Appointment, renewal and termination filings are manual through the regulator portal; the catalog integrates filings directly with NIPR/PDB, reducing cycle time and transcription error.

critical_missing.title: Automated CE synchronisation
critical_missing.detail: CE is verified by manual transcript review; the catalog auto-syncs CE completions from providers.

control_bars.name: Termination
control_bars.note: For-cause handling and withdrawal filing solid

remediations.title: Implement continuous compliance monitoring
remediations.detail: Subscribe to adverse-action, watchlist and licence-standing feeds so disqualifying events between renewals are flagged and dispositioned. Effort: 5 weeks (AgentOS + feeds).

remediations.title: Enforce a ready-to-sell gate at point of sale
remediations.detail: Block quote/bind in PAS for any producer not currently licensed and appointed for the product and territory. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Automate regulator filings via NIPR/PDB integration
remediations.detail: Replace manual portal filings with direct appointment/renewal/termination filing integration. Effort: 6 weeks.

remediations.title: Automate CE synchronisation
remediations.detail: Auto-sync CE completions from approved providers and flag shortfalls without manual transcript review. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Add producer self-service onboarding portal
remediations.detail: Let producers submit documents, sign agreements and track status in a self-service portal. Effort: 5 weeks.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Sircon Producer Lifecycle Management Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v2.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-005. Alignment to the Sircon producer lifecycle catalog increased from 72% to 92%. Critical additions: continuous compliance monitoring, a real-time ready-to-sell gate at point of sale, automated regulator filing (NIPR/PDB), automated CE synchronisation, and continuous licence verification.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the Sircon catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional automation or controls

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the process for licensing, renewing, amending and terminating insurance distribution agreements with agents and brokers. Version 2.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Sircon Producer Lifecycle Management Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 92% alignment (up from 72% in v1.4).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to new agent/broker appointment and licensing, annual renewals and CE compliance, licence amendments, and voluntary or for-cause terminations.
Scope extended: continuous compliance monitoring and a real-time ready-to-sell gate now apply to every active producer at all times.
This procedure does NOT cover in-house staff licensing (HR onboarding SOP) or MGA entity licensing above the defined book threshold (SOP-INS-015).

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Completes application and training; maintains active licence and CE; notifies of status changes.
Verifies and syncs licences; files appointments/renewals/terminations; manages records.
Maintains contracting files; executes agreements; manages contract version control.
Reviews screening; owns continuous monitoring dispositions and ongoing standing.

sections.title: 5. Continuous Compliance (New Section — v2.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the Sircon catalog. Continuous monitoring and a ready-to-sell gate now operate for every active producer, not only at appointment and renewal.
Ready-to-sell gate at point of sale

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Compliance reviews specific findings; minor adverse history may be approved with documented justification.
Material adverse findings (e.g. fraud conviction, prior licence revocation) result in automatic rejection.
All adverse findings and decisions are logged in the Compliance tracking register.
The ready-to-sell gate now blocks sales the moment a licence lapses, rather than detecting lapse sales after the fact.
On expiry, the producer's ready-to-sell flag is revoked and quote/bind is blocked in PAS immediately.
Reinstatement is initiated; outstanding CE must be completed within 30 days.
Any sales attempted during the lapse are blocked at point of sale; historical lapse exposure is eliminated.
New exception. A mid-term adverse-action or watchlist hit triggers immediate Compliance review.
Monitoring alert routes to Compliance for disposition within 1 business day.
Disqualifying events trigger immediate ready-to-sell suspension pending review.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Licence tracking, continuous sync, filing status
Pre-appointment and CE training and sync

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v2.0: continuous monitoring, ready-to-sell enforcement, automated regulator filing and CE synchronisation.
Continuous monitoring: adverse-action and watchlist events flagged between renewals.
Ready-to-sell gate: no quote/bind for unlicensed or unappointed producers.
Automated filing: appointment/renewal/termination filed via regulator integration with audit trail.
CE synchronisation: completions auto-synced; shortfalls flagged before renewal.
Training gate: mandatory AML/conduct/product training before any appointment filing.
Segregation of duties: appointment filing ≠ compliance disposition.
Immutable audit trail: verification, screening, filing and monitoring events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Aligned to revised DOA; updated DocuSign execution steps
OPTIMISED: continuous compliance monitoring; ready-to-sell gate; automated NIPR/PDB filing; CE synchronisation; continuous licence verification; catalog alignment 72% → 92%
